results.title: [論文評述] PianoKontext: Expressive Performance Rendering from Deadpan Context
results.content:
PianoKontext significantly outperforms the CFG Bridge baseline across all evaluation metrics. It achieves lower FAD (2.96 vs. 4.69) and KAD (0.91 vs. 1.68), indicating higher audio fidelity. It also demonstrates superior content preservation with a higher Pitch DTW (0.888 vs. 0.856), and improved note-level accuracy with higher Alignment Precision (0.630 vs. 0.466) and Recall (0.666 vs. 0.373). Human performance metrics are provided as upper bounds for context. The model successfully generates performances with varying tempos, showcasing its controllability via different duration factors initialized during inference. While effective in preserving structure, harmony, and melody, the model's ability to render nuanced articulation (e.g., non-legato passages) is identified as a limitation. [...] Moonlight Logo

# [論文評述] PianoKontext: Expressive Performance Rendering from Deadpan Context

PianoKontext addresses the challenge of Expressive Performance Rendering (EPR) for classical piano music, which involves generating realistic musical performances constrained by note sequences. Existing flow matching audio editing models typically manipulate synchronized music samples of fixed duration, hindering their ability to understand and generate expressive timing. This paper introduces PianoKontext, a novel flow matching rendering model that operates in the latent space of a pretrained Music2Latent model to generate variable-length expressive performances from "deadpan" (non-expressive) contexts. [...] (e.g., non-legato passages) is identified as a limitation. Future work aims to explore other instruments, rigorously evaluate musicality, and extend generation to full-length performances through techniques like outpainting.

results.title: Expressive Performance Rendering from Deadpan Context - arXiv
results.content:
Expressive performance rendering (EPR) aims to generate realistic performances constrained on sequences of notes. However, flow matching audio editing models manipulate only synchronized music samples of the same duration, limiting their understanding of expressive timing. We introduce PianoKontext, a flow matching rendering model for classical piano music that generates variable-length performances in the latent space of a pretrained Music2Latent model. We synthesize MIDI scores into deadpan audio and employ Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) in the latent space to construct paired data for training. The aligned embeddings are concatenated in DiT blocks, allowing for a simple and effective learning of the dependencies between the score and performances. Audio samples are available at our demo [...] ## 5 Results and Discussion

Table 2 presents the results of the evaluation. PianoKontext outperforms an unsupervised bridge method in audio fidelity (FAD, KAD) and content preservation (Pitch DTW, Alignment Precision, Alignment Recall). Human-level Pitch DTW indicates the preservation of structure and harmony and can be seen as a ”soft” metric. The precision and recall, which are ”hard” due to their discrete nature, suggest that PianoKontext is significantly less prone to deviations from the score compared to CFG Bridge. Since the transcription model is not perfect, we also provide the metrics for human performances that serve as upper bounds. [...] In this work, we propose PianoKontext, a latent flow matching model that renders a variable-length piano performance segments given a deadpan latent context. Our framework is inspired by FLUX Kontext (Labs et al., 2025), an image editing model that jointly models the dependencies between the given context and target images with self-attention in Diffusion Transformer (DiT) blocks (Peebles and Xie, 2023). Similarly, we synthesize score MIDI into audio with a simple soundfont to serve as a deadpan context. We introduce a data preprocessing step based on Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) (Sakoe and Chiba, 1970) that enables an effective sampling of paired score-performance data.
